function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_osint_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the OSINT category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_osint_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the OSINT system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for gau (unknown/gau). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for gau. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

documentation.how_to_use:
Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/gau && python3 gau.py --help`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/gau && cat README.md`; `cd toolboxes/gau && ls -la`. getallurls (gau)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-_red.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).
documentation.when_to_use:
Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web] in the OSINT category. Fits when the engagement needs: getallurls (gau)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-_red.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/waybackurls (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/phoneinfoga (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with unknown/meg (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
